title: Cardano's Van Rossem Hard Fork Cuts Smart Contract Costs, Preps for Leios
summary: Cardano has activated its Van Rossem hard fork, reducing smart contract costs and paving the way for a major scalability upgrade called Ouroboros Leios later this year. This move aims to make Cardano more competitive in the blockchain space.
body:
Cardano has successfully activated the Van Rossem hard fork, a significant upgrade that reduces the cost of executing smart contracts on its blockchain. This update also lays the groundwork for Ouroboros Leios, a major scalability upgrade expected later this year.

The Van Rossem hard fork introduces several improvements, including optimized smart contract execution and reduced transaction fees. Ouroboros Leios, the next big upgrade, is designed to enhance the network's throughput and efficiency, making Cardano more competitive with other blockchain platforms.

For everyday users, this upgrade means lower costs for using decentralized applications (dApps) and smart contracts on the Cardano network. Developers will also benefit from improved tools and lower operational costs, potentially leading to more innovative projects on the platform.

The Cardano community is closely watching for the rollout of Ouroboros Leios, which promises to significantly boost the network's capacity. Users and developers should keep an eye on upcoming announcements for more details on this major upgrade.
twitterThread: Cardano just cut smart contract costs with its Van Rossem hard fork, setting the stage for a big scalability boost later this year. ⚡
The upgrade optimizes smart contract execution and reduces fees, while paving the way for Ouroboros Leios. Developers and users save money.

Watch for Ouroboros Leios later this year—it could make Cardano a top contender. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-20-cardanos-van-rossem-hard-fork-cuts-smart-contract-costs-preps-for-leios
